post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 170: The Mafia Invitational!


If you are dead or are not in this game, please do not post in this thread.


Please note that this is an invitational game. Each of you invited holds the rank of SE or IC. You all should know the importance of being active. Replacing out is highly discouraged as replacements will be difficult to find, so do not do so unless you really have no other choice. You have been invited due to meeting certain standards, and I hope that you will continue to meet them in this game.

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
One day ten scrubs got together to play mafia. A certain jeff was less scrubby and brought a gun to shoot scrubs with. A certain jony and bean became extra scrubby and decided to shoot all the less scrubby people. Then ShineBunny wrote this crap flavor so he died.

[obligatory mod and co-mod have died stuff]


General Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or co-moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes but is not limited to small font size and white-colored text.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, do so before submitting your post into the thread.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
  • Do not attempt to abuse or exploit any of the rules.


Voting/Vote-kills
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: ShineBunny" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: ShineBunny", "Vote ShineBunny", "vote: ShineBunny", "Vote:ShineBunny", "Vote: SheinBunny", "Vote: ShinBnuny", "Vote: shinebunny", Vote: scrub and "Vote: ShineBunny" are not.
  • Spell and capitalize the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames. Do not attempt to abuse this rule.
  • You may vote for no vote-kill to occur during a day. Do so by posting "Vote: Skip Day".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • This game is based on deadline vote-kills. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is vote-killed. In the case of a tie, the vote-kill is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority vote-kill is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If a majority vote-kill is not reached, a deadline vote-kill occurs.
  • Votes are locked during LyLo. This means that if the number of mafia alive is exactly one less than the number of townspeople alive during the day, any votes made are final and cannot be unvoted.
  • If at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for Skip Day, then there will be no vote-kill. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post. It will then be twilight.
  • During twilight, players may not post.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person vote-killed (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity/Day/Night
  • Each day phase will last 264 hours (11 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 264 hours have elapsed since the start of day.
  • Each night phase will last for 72 hours.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day. Do not post until the moderator makes the post announcing the end of night, even if the deadline for night passes.
  • If a player does not make any game-advancing posts in a 24-hour period and he/she is not on V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not make any game-advancing posts in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not on V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not on V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • Each player must make at least 15 contentful posts per day phase. Failure to do so will result in a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.
  • If a player receives a total of 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • If any player commits an excessive amount of rule infractions, they will be force-replaced. If the infraction committed is severe enough and impacts the game to an extent where force-replacing would not solve the problem, such as a player posting a screenshot of their Role PM in thread, I will be forced to modkill that player. Please do not make me modkill you as it affects the game adversely.


Moderators
  • I (ShineBunny) am the Moderator of Game 170. atomicpianowitch is the co-moderator of Game 170.
  • Red-colored text (#f00) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#00f) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#38761D) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This game will use the Vig 10'er setup. There is 1 Town Vigilante, 7 Vanilla Townies, and 2 Mafia Goons. The Vigilante's kill is optional. The Mafia factional kill is not optional; in case of no nightkill being submitted, the target will be randomized.

The Mafia team will have a QuickTopic thread which they can communicate in at any time.

Role PMs
scrub (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are a scrub (Vanilla Townie). You are so scrubby that the only things you possess are your annoying voice and your vote. The link to the game thread is here.

You win when all the inactive ICs (Mafia) are dead.
jeffisepic (Town Vigilante) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are jeffisepic (Town Vigilante). You are a living legend, so you can get rid of a scrub every night. This kill is optional. The link to the game thread is here.

You win when all the inactive ICs (Mafia) are dead.
{jonyj1005, abean077} (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are an inactive IC ({jonyj1005, abean077}, Mafia Goon). You are a huge scrub who is too "busy" to play anymore. You have come back to roast other scrubs, but do not realize that you yourself are even scrubbier now. Your fellow inactive IC is [player].

You share a factional kill with your partner, which is not optional. In case you do not submit an action before the deadline, your kill will be randomized. You can communicate with your partner at any time in this QuickTopic.

You win when the number of inactive ICs (Mafia) is greater than or equal to the number of townspeople alive, or when nothing can prevent the same.


Alive (7)
  1. jeffisepic++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
  2. abean077++
  3. e_is_cool++
  4. summitwei++
  5. statisticslol++ tikkitamatoa
  6. pandabear10+ GeneralCobra19+
  7. jeremylu+


Dead (3)
  1. * SFGiantsFan, inactive IC abean077 (Mafia Goon), was roasted to death Day 1.
  2. * fdas, scrub (Vanilla Townie), was defenestrated Night 1.
  3. * chess24, inactive IC jonyj1005 (Mafia Goon), died playing Russian roulette Night 1.


Significant Events
* tikkitamatoa replaces statisticslol
* GeneralCobra19 replaces pandabear10
* fdas replaces whatshisbucket
* SFGiantsFan, inactive IC abean077 (Mafia Goon), is hanged Day 1.
* fdas, scrub (Vanilla Townie), is killed Night 1.
* chess24, inactive IC jonyj1005 (Mafia Goon), is killed Night 1.


Warnings
[warnings hopefully not coming soon]



Do not post until this line is crossed out.

You may now post. Day 1 begins now and ends at 9:34 PM PDT on August 27.

i don't think just because people have a read, means that they should try to get other people to agree with them. maybe they're not sure about their read themselves and think they have a high chance of being wrong, maybe they want to let what's happening continue for the moment because data is maximized this way, maybe they would prefer to take a more passive-aggressive approach by putting their reasons out there and seeing what happens. Again, scum works with possibility, while town works with probability.
Oh boy I've seen this line from you before and never gotten the chance to roast it. Let's go

Let's talk about possibility and probability. By probability, you are thinking about "what is likely", and by possibility, you think about "what is possible". Thinking about what is likely makes sense as any alignment. As town, you want to hang likely wolves, not just possible ones. As a wolf in a cop9 setup, you want to nightkill likely cops, not just the possible ones. The examples are endless.
Thinking about what is possible also makes sense as any alignment. You're not going to be right every time, that's just a fact of life. When you are right, it feels great, sure, but when you're wrong you need to find somewhere to go. What happens when your top scumread flips town, where do you go? Are you completely screwed if you kill this one player and they're not the cop? Possibilities are important to consider from any alignment.

I believe that possibility and probability are, frankly like most things, a matter of personality rather than alignment.
Some people are greedy optimists. They will take the path that they believe wins the most without regard for the risk it runs if they're wrong. This is done as either alignment.
I think of myself, on the other hand, as a realist. In a newbie game, I prefer not hanging experienced players on D1, even if I scumread them. This is because in the, let's say, 40% chance that I'm wrong, town just killed one of their best players who could have possible been the nightkill, and has nowhere really to go.
Of course, everything is done in context. Sometimes I make seemingly stupid plays, or the optimistic ones, because I believe them to be the only way I ever win. This is something I do as either alignment, and I'd be happy to give past examples. But in general I tend to be an extremely "safe" player, probably too safe actually.

summitwei wrote:
Oh boy I've seen this line from you before and never gotten the chance to roast it. Let's go

Let's talk about possibility and probability. By probability, you are thinking about "what is likely", and by possibility, you think about "what is possible". Thinking about what is likely makes sense as any alignment. As town, you want to hang likely wolves, not just possible ones. As a wolf in a cop9 setup, you want to nightkill likely cops, not just the possible ones. The examples are endless.
Thinking about what is possible also makes sense as any alignment. You're not going to be right every time, that's just a fact of life. When you are right, it feels great, sure, but when you're wrong you need to find somewhere to go. What happens when your top scumread flips town, where do you go? Are you completely screwed if you kill this one player and they're not the cop? Possibilities are important to consider from any alignment.

I believe that possibility and probability are, frankly like most things, a matter of personality rather than alignment.
Some people are greedy optimists. They will take the path that they believe wins the most without regard for the risk it runs if they're wrong. This is done as either alignment.
I think of myself, on the other hand, as a realist. In a newbie game, I prefer not hanging experienced players on D1, even if I scumread them. This is because in the, let's say, 40% chance that I'm wrong, town just killed one of their best players who could have possible been the nightkill, and has nowhere really to go.
Of course, everything is done in context. Sometimes I make seemingly stupid plays, or the optimistic ones, because I believe them to be the only way I ever win. This is something I do as either alignment, and I'd be happy to give past examples. But in general I tend to be an extremely "safe" player, probably too safe actually.

chess24

I don't expect I'll have fun with this read. If chess is scum, he's trying to pocket me and frankly he's not doing that terrible of a job. Could probably do with taking whatever read I end up on him and subtracting a few town points for this.

The thing I find interesting about chess in the summit/e threadwar: chess virtually never talks to e, but sometimes talks directly to me, the most notable of which being the third quote in 300. I find this interesting for several reasons. This one-sided question feels out of place. Why ask me to stop tunneling e but never ask e to stop tunneling me back (especially when e is a townlean)? If there's a reason for this I'd love to hear it but as of now I think this might just be a pocket or otherwise just trying to form interactions that help his partner if he flips.
chess's other notable action is his visible uncertainty in 311 and similar posts. I could go and contemplate whether or not this is genuine, but to be honest I don't think I'd get anywhere on that.
So let's go back to cold analysis. I haven't done an in-depth study on chess's meta (namely I haven't read a town chess game) but I read 166. chess in that game displays quite a bit of confidence - there's only one post that wavers and it's this one at the end, which is still debatable. I don't know if any of this is alignment indicative, but it's probably interesting to note regardless.
chess's jeff attack... confuses me further. I frankly don't see what scum!chess is doing attacking jeff, that is, what he's trying to accomplish. It doesn't make him look towny, it doesn't push someone who's close to being hanged. I can't fathom a scum agenda behind this read. I want to town read it so badly... but the idea that scum!chess was desperately trying to shift the status quo and did something stupid for that very purpose is tugging at me.

chess as a slot confuses me heavily, but I think I'd put him overall at a scum lean. I could easily see him flipping town, and there are even some things I'm so close to saying don't come from scum, but overall I feel like the victim of a pocketing attempt and this puts me in not a very happy mood while making this read. Add some inconsistency throughout, which is quite scummy, and that's where I'm at. This could very easily change for better or for worse depending on chess's replies and his reads as a whole - we'll just have to see then.

I haven't done an in-depth study on chess's meta (namely I haven't read a town chess game) but I read 166. chess in that game displays quite a bit of confidence - there's only one post that wavers and it's [url=http://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h1647456p10607682]this one[/url] at the end, which is still debatable. I don't know if any of this is alignment indicative, but it's probably interesting to note regardless.
chess's jeff attack... confuses me further. I frankly don't see what scum!chess is doing attacking jeff, that is, what he's trying to accomplish. It doesn't make him look towny, it doesn't push someone who's close to being hanged. I can't fathom a scum agenda behind this read. I want to town read it so badly... but the idea that scum!chess was desperately trying to shift the status quo and did something stupid for that very purpose is tugging at me.

chess as a slot confuses me heavily, but I think I'd put him overall at a [b]scum lean[/b]. I could easily see him flipping town, and there are even some things I'm so close to saying don't come from scum, but overall I feel like the victim of a pocketing attempt and this puts me in not a very happy mood while making this read. Add some inconsistency throughout, which is quite scummy, and that's where I'm at. This could very easily change for better or for worse depending on chess's replies and his reads as a whole - we'll just have to see then.

This. This is the slot I've been waiting through all these reads to talk about. I started getting some bad vibes while reading through, and this ISO has only confirmed those suspicions.
I find some interesting things in SFGF's posts, particularly regarding the summit/chess interaction. 91's last bit is something I think very rarely comes from someone who has any idea how interactions work, and I think SFGF belongs to this subset of people.
He does some walls on chess/summit counterwagons, sure, we all know SFGF loves counterwagoning. I find it very interesting, however, that SFGF is always pushing either summit/chess w/w or w/v - i.e. he rarely considers chess's alignment in a world where I flip town. He even claims it makes chess townier - in the aforementioned 91, and subsequent defenses, SFGF even says "if summit is town chess looks a bit better" which makes no sense whatsoever and feels like a bad excuse to read a partner town after a town flip. This makes SFGF/chess look more likely, and I think it makes SFGF look badly regardless of chess's flip.
SFGF's wallposts have agenda written all over them. There's this one passage I find the most blatant example of this, and it's in 288.
SFGiantsFan wrote:
it seems obvious to me that summit and e aren't scumpartners so if summit flips town then I would consider a world where scum!e exists. Right now I read this as town!e is tunneling so hard on scum!summit that he refuses to read his posts anymore
chess pointed this out and even though I think they might be w/w I think it's a valid point. How do you read me and e differently for doing the same actions? And most importantly, why are you changing views after a town flip when you're not directly involved in the thread war? SFGF is spelling out his wolf agenda here - hang summit, and only then consider e wolf, hang e, ggez. He also had a summit v -> tikki w read which makes very little sense and I think the most likely explanation was that he needed somewhere to go if jeff and the like remained adamant on e being town, like a back-up plan or something.
The middle of 351 confused me for a bit because I had to think if this is possibly w/w... and I think it still can be. At the very least, I would expect SFGF to remove his current vote once chess did his elaboration (not sure if he will anymore if they are wolfing together so lmao).

SFGF's posts feel extremely agenda-driven, and he has said things that are illogical in a way that comes from scum and not from town (I'm usually pretty bad at distinguishing these usually but I believe this time I'm right). Include some sketchy interactions particularly with chess who is also relatively scummy, and this has ended me off with a scum read.

post_rendered:
Ok so after the e/summit wallposts, I quickly realized if I wanted to fully understand what was going on this game, I would have to spend way more time than I wanted to. As a result, instead of poking holes at any arguments and playing my aggro-reaction based style, I just tried to post enough that I wouldn't be lurking and then have enough reads that I wouldn't be wishy washy or anything. Also, its much harder to push experienced players compared to newbs who tend to lose composure faster.

I'm going to be honest half the posts I didn't even read in this game. If it was too long it just wasn't worth my time. Also, AoPS formatting is disgusting so hide posts are really difficult to read. To this day I have no idea why chess was scummy early in day 1. So I just tried to act logic based which sometimes led to me not knowing what to say or freezing as jeff said. Sometimes I didn't post because I felt like I would be repeating what others said.

EOD was probably one of the most fun times for me playing mafia. Besides an obvious skill difference compared to other games, I think the second biggest factor in my overall enjoyment was the activity. When everybody is posting and participating the game actually goes places. I don't know how to close so yea.
post_canonical:
